_scope: Live clients (company_status_type_id = 4) with >=500 POs in last 12 months. Ranked by rejection_rate_pct. Pattern field classifies rejection-note text into duplicate / coaching / policy / ownership / mixed / insufficient.
_notes: This is the CS Toolkit's core targeting list. Top of table = highest CSM leverage per outreach hour.
EPC Real Estate Group is a massive outlier — 45.5% rejection rate is 2.5x the next client and >8x the suite average of ~3%.
IRT rejects only 8.4% but is the biggest volume — 9,502 rejected POs = the largest absolute waste in the book.

Pattern is derived from top-note ILIKE classification: duplicate = 'duplicate' / 'invoice already entered' / etc.; coaching = 'pls code' / 'add unit number' / 'attach documentation' / etc.; policy = 'do not use POs' / 'we don't use'; ownership = 'no longer manage' / 'community sold'. 'Insufficient signal' means fewer than 5% of rejection notes at the client matched any classifier bucket.

pattern_legend.coaching-heavy: Rejection notes are approver corrections (GL codes, unit numbers, supporting docs). Product-fixable via entry-form validation. Design partner candidate.
pattern_legend.duplicate-heavy: Rejection notes are 'duplicate PO' or 'invoice already entered'. Product-fixable via create-time duplicate detection.
pattern_legend.policy-heavy: Rejection notes indicate client has decided not to use POs (or a portfolio of theirs hasn't). Not a product problem — CSM conversation about workflow alignment.
pattern_legend.ownership-heavy: Rejection notes indicate property ownership changes (community sold, no longer manage). Data hygiene / property lifecycle problem.
pattern_legend.mixed: Multiple patterns present, no single dominant one. Deep-dive recommended before outreach.
pattern_legend.insufficient signal: Fewer than 5% of rejection notes match any classifier bucket. Client's approvers write free-form notes we can't categorize without deep-dive.

rankings.company_name: EPC Real Estate Group, LLC dba EPC Living
rankings.cs_theme: Systemic workflow break — investigate approver routing
rankings.pattern_note: 191 rejections cite 'do not use POs' vs. 90 duplicate mentions — 5 KC-area properties opted out of POs. Deep-dive available.

rankings.cs_theme: Elevated — likely coding or routing pattern
rankings.pattern_note: Only 4% of rejection notes match classifiers — approvers write free-form context. Needs deep-dive.
3% classifier hit rate — approvers appear to write ad-hoc explanations. Deep-dive to characterize.

rankings.cs_theme: High volume + high rate — biggest quick-win $
rankings.pattern_note: 849 of 859 classified rejections (98.8%) are coaching notes. Single approver (user 234392, Accountant) does 90% of rejections. Design partner candidate. Deep-dive available.
73 coaching notes vs. 14 duplicate mentions (12.8% signal). Coaching dominant but low classifier hit rate — some deep-dive risk.
Duplicate 29, coaching 18 out of 47 classified — no dominant pattern. Deep-dive recommended before framing outreach.
511 of 537 classified rejections (95%) are coaching. Consistent with 'training gap' hypothesis. Strong design partner candidate.
Only 2% classifier hit rate — needs deep-dive. Volume warrants the effort.

rankings.cs_theme: Student housing complexity — likely GL misalignment
rankings.pattern_note: 211 rejections cite 'no longer manage' / 'community sold' (70% of 302 classified). Data-hygiene problem, not product-fixable at entry form. CSM path: property lifecycle cleanup.
5% classifier hit rate — deep-dive to characterize the approval loop.
43 of 43 classified rejections are coaching. 100% of the classified signal, though only 35% of all rejections classified. Directional match.
Zero classified rejections; all 55 notes are unmatched free-form. Volume too low to prioritize.

rankings.cs_theme: LARGEST VOLUME — 9,502 rejected POs = biggest $ recovery opportunity in the book
rankings.pattern_note: Deep-dive revealed 4-pattern shape: 350 duplicate + 261 GL coding + 199 budget-guardrail (NEW — Advanced Budgeting integration story) + 180 turn/timing dates (NEW). Multi-tier approval org — 8 Regional Managers do 51% of rejections. Design-partner candidate, not rescue account. Deep-dive available.
69 duplicate / 33 coaching out of 102 classified (68% dup dominant). Create-time duplicate detection is the direct product intervention.
22 policy / 12 duplicate out of 34 classified (65% policy). Smaller-scale EPC-shape — deep-dive to confirm before outreach.

rankings.cs_theme: Mid-market volume + rework — approval chain review
rankings.pattern_note: 264 coaching / 45 duplicate / 10 policy out of 319 classified (83% coaching). Design partner candidate at scale.
11 coaching / 6 duplicate — low absolute count. Directional coaching pattern.
472 coaching / 29 duplicate / 8 policy out of 509 classified (93% coaching). Enterprise-scale coaching pattern. Design partner.
3.7% classifier hit rate. At low volume — spot-check only.
Only 2 classified rejections. Volume too low.
83 coaching / 6 duplicate out of 89 classified (93% coaching). Modest signal but consistent.

rankings.cs_theme: Enterprise — volume alone puts them on radar
rankings.pattern_note: 192 coaching / 168 duplicate / 34 ownership / 10 policy out of 404 classified. Coaching (48%) and duplicate (42%) both present without a clear leader. Enterprise-scale mix — deep-dive.
6.5% classifier hit rate — approvers write free-form. Deep-dive if bandwidth allows.
30 of 30 classified rejections are duplicate (100%). Cleanest duplicate-pattern client in the top-25. Direct match for the create-time duplicate detection epic.
360 of 363 classified rejections (99%) are coaching. Enterprise-scale coaching pattern. Design partner.
